# Story 1.3: implementar-comando-pipeline-ingest-orquestrador-basico

Status: review

## Story

As a User (CLI),
I want a `pipeline.ingest --ticker <TICKER> --source <provider>` command,
so that I can trigger an end-to-end ingest using the minimal adapter and mapper.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the `yfinance` adapter and canonical mapper are available, when the user runs `pipeline.ingest --source yfinance PETR4.SA` (options must precede ticker), then the pipeline fetches raw data via adapter and normalizes via mapper.  The CLI wrapper validates the provider name and emits a clear error if unknown.
2. The command saves provider raw CSV (see Story 1.4) and persists canonical rows to DB when persistence stories (1.6) are implemented.
3. The command returns a clear success/failure exit code and logs a `job_id` for the run.
4. CLI supports flags `--dry-run` (no writes) and `--force-refresh` (ignore cache).
5. Errors are logged to `ingest_logs` with descriptive messages and non-zero exit code on unrecoverable failures.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Implement CLI command entrypoint `pipeline.ingest` (Typer)
  - [x] Subtask 1.1: Wire CLI to orchestration function `pipeline.ingest_command`
  - [x] Subtask 1.2: Add `--ticker`, `--source`, `--dry-run`, `--force-refresh` flags and validation
- [x] Task 2: Orchestrator implementation
  - [x] Subtask 2.1: Call `Adapter.fetch()` for provider
  - [x] Subtask 2.2: Call Canonical Mapper to normalize
  - [x] Subtask 2.3: When not `--dry-run`, trigger raw save (Story 1.4) and DB persist (Story 1.6)
  - [x] Subtask 2.4: Emit structured logs including `job_id`, timings and row counts
- [x] Task 3: CLI integration tests (smoke)
  - [x] Subtask 3.1: Add a mocked adapter fixture to run ingest in `--dry-run` and assert orchestration flow
- [x] Documentar o que foi implantado nessa etapa em `docs/sprint-reports` conforme definido no FR28 (`docs/planning-artifacts/prd.md`)

## Dev Notes

- Arquitetura: Typer-based CLI; orchestration layer `pipeline` sits under `src.pipeline` (ex.: `src/pipeline/__init__.py`, `src/pipeline/ingest.py`).
- Dependências/stack: `pandas`, `sqlalchemy` (SQLite), `typer`, `pandera` (validation), `python-dotenv` (env), `pytest` for tests.
- Persistence: when persistence stories are implemented, use `dados/data.db` with `prices` table and upsert by `(ticker, date)`.
- Files written during runs: `raw/<provider>/<ticker>-<ts>.csv` and `ingest_logs` entry; recommend `raw/` under project root.
- Security: do not commit provider API keys; read from env vars (.env) and advise `.env.example`.

### Project Structure Notes

- Suggested layout:
  - src/pipeline/ingest.py  # orchestrator and CLI bindings
  - src/adapters/           # provider adapters (yfinance, alphavantage)
  - src/mappers/canonical.py
  - src/db/                 # DB layer and upsert helpers
  - raw/<provider>/         # raw CSV outputs
  - dados/data.db           # SQLite DB

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

GPT-5 mini

### Completion Notes List

- CLI command `pipeline ingest` implemented as Typer subcommand; ticker is positional and options (`--source`, `--dry-run`, `--force-refresh`) must appear before the ticker value.
- Orchestrator logic handles fetch, mapping, raw save, and invokes incremental persistence helper; errors are captured and logged.
- Added Typer/Click 3.14 compatibility patch to prevent help-generation crash.
- Created `src/pipeline.py` module and new `tests/test_pipeline_cli.py` with integration-style tests.
- Metadata logging for each run written to JSONL; job_id printed by CLI.
- Added sprint-report draft describing implementation (see separate file).
